toolUseResult.content:
# Auth API — Sequence Diagrams

All flows use these participants:

- **Client** — Frontend / other microservice
- **RackAttack** — Rate-limiting middleware (Redis-backed)
- **AuthController** — `API::V1::AuthenticationController`
- **JWTService** — Token encode/decode + revocation check
- **TokenDenylist** — Redis wrapper for JTI + user-level revocation
- **PasswordValidator** — NIST 800-63B checks (length, breach, history, similarity)
- **HIBP** — `api.pwnedpasswords.com` k-anonymity API
- **DB** — PostgreSQL (AuthCredential, RefreshToken, tokens, history)
- **Redis** — Token denylist + rate-limit counters
- **RabbitMQ** — Async event bus → notification-service

---

## 1. Register

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant RackAttack
    participant AuthController
    participant PasswordValidator
    participant HIBP
    participant DB
    participant JWTService
    participant RabbitMQ

    Client->>RackAttack: POST /api/v1/auth/register
    RackAttack-->>RackAttack: check registrations/ip (3/hour)
    alt rate limit exceeded
        RackAttack-->>Client: 429 Too Many Requests
    end

    RackAttack->>AuthController: pass request

    AuthController->>PasswordValidator: validate(password, email)
    PasswordValidator-->>PasswordValidator: length 14–64 chars
    PasswordValidator-->>PasswordValidator: not in common_passwords.txt
    PasswordValidator-->>PasswordValidator: similarity check vs email (Levenshtein < 0.7)
    PasswordValidator->>HIBP: GET /range/{sha1_prefix} (k-anonymity)
    HIBP-->>PasswordValidator: list of matching suffixes
    PasswordValidator-->>PasswordValidator: check suffix in response
    alt password invalid
        PasswordValidator-->>AuthController: validation errors
        AuthController-->>Client: 422 Unprocessable Entity
    end

    AuthController->>DB: INSERT auth_credentials (email, bcrypt_digest, role=user)
    DB-->>AuthController: AuthCredential record

    AuthController->>JWTService: generate_access_token(credential)
    JWTService-->>AuthController: JWT {sub, email, role, jti, iat, exp=15min, iss}

    AuthController->>DB: INSERT refresh_tokens (token, expires_at=+8h, last_used_at=now)
    DB-->>AuthController: RefreshToken record

    AuthController->>DB: INSERT email_verification_tokens (token, expires_at=+24h)
    DB-->>AuthController: EmailVerificationToken record

    AuthController->>RabbitMQ: publish(email.verification, {to, verify_url, expires_in})
    RabbitMQ-->>AuthController: ack

    AuthController-->>Client: 201 {user, access_token, refresh_token}
```

---

## 2. Login

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant RackAttack
    participant AuthController
    participant DB
    participant JWTService

    Client->>RackAttack: POST /api/v1/auth/login {email, password}
    RackAttack-->>RackAttack: check logins/ip (5/min)
    RackAttack-->>RackAttack: check logins/email (5/min)
    RackAttack-->>RackAttack: check logins/email/lockout (10/hour)
    alt rate limit exceeded
        RackAttack-->>Client: 429 Too Many Requests + Retry-After header
    end

    RackAttack->>AuthController: pass request

    AuthController->>DB: SELECT auth_credentials WHERE email = ?
    DB-->>AuthController: credential or nil

    alt credential not found
        AuthController-->>Client: 401 Invalid credentials
    end

    AuthController-->>AuthController: credential.authenticate(password) — bcrypt compare
    alt password mismatch
        AuthController-->>Client: 401 Invalid credentials
    end

    alt account deactivated
        AuthController-->>Client: 403 Account deactivated
    end

    AuthController->>JWTService: generate_access_token(credential)
    JWTService-->>AuthController: signed JWT (exp=15min)

    AuthController->>DB: INSERT refresh_tokens (token, expires_at=+8h, last_used_at=now)
    DB-->>AuthController: RefreshToken record

    AuthController-->>Client: 200 {user, access_token, refresh_token}
```

---

## 3. Logout

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant AuthController
    participant JWTService
    participant TokenDenylist
    participant Redis
    participant DB

    Client->>AuthController: POST /api/v1/auth/logout\nAuthorization: Bearer <access_token>\n{refresh_token}

    AuthController->>JWTService: decode(access_token)
    JWTService->>Redis: GET jti:denylist:{jti}
    Redis-->>JWTService: nil (not revoked)
    JWTService->>Redis: GET user:tokens:invalidated:{user_id}
    Redis-->>JWTService: nil (not invalidated)
    JWTService-->>AuthController: {sub, jti, exp, ...}

    AuthController->>DB: SELECT auth_credentials WHERE id = sub
    DB-->>AuthController: AuthCredential

    AuthController->>DB: SELECT refresh_tokens WHERE token = ?
    DB-->>AuthController: RefreshToken

    alt refresh token not found or wrong owner
        AuthController-->>Client: 404 / 403
    end

    AuthController->>DB: UPDATE refresh_tokens SET revoked_at = now
    DB-->>AuthController: ok

    AuthController->>TokenDenylist: revoke!(jti, exp)
    TokenDenylist->>Redis: SETEX jti:denylist:{jti} {ttl} "1"
    Redis-->>TokenDenylist: ok

    AuthController-->>Client: 200 {message: "Logged out successfully"}
```

---

## 4. Token Refresh

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant RackAttack
    participant AuthController
    participant DB
    participant JWTService

    Client->>RackAttack: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh {refresh_token}
    RackAttack-->>RackAttack: check refresh/ip (10/min)
    alt rate limit exceeded
        RackAttack-->>Client: 429 Too Many Requests
    end

    RackAttack->>AuthController: pass request

    AuthController->>DB: SELECT refresh_tokens WHERE token = ?
    DB-->>AuthController: RefreshToken + AuthCredential

    alt token not found
        AuthController-->>Client: 401 Invalid or expired refresh token
    end

    AuthController-->>AuthController: token.valid_for_use?\n(!expired? && !revoked? && !idle_timed_out?)
    alt token invalid
        AuthController-->>Client: 401 Invalid or expired refresh token
    end

    AuthController->>DB: UPDATE refresh_tokens SET last_used_at = now
    DB-->>AuthController: ok

    AuthController->>JWTService: generate_access_token(credential)
    JWTService-->>AuthController: new JWT (exp=15min, new jti)

    AuthController-->>Client: 200 {access_token, user}
```

---

## 5. Validate Token (inter-service)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Microservice
    participant AuthController
    participant JWTService
    participant Redis

    Microservice->>AuthController: POST /api/v1/auth/validate\nAuthorization: Bearer <token>

    AuthController->>JWTService: decode(token)
    JWTService-->>JWTService: verify signature (HS256)
    JWTService-->>JWTService: verify exp, iat, iss=auth-api
    JWTService->>Redis: GET jti:denylist:{jti}
    Redis-->>JWTService: nil / "1"
    JWTService->>Redis: GET user:tokens:invalidated:{sub}
    Redis-->>JWTService: nil / timestamp

    alt token valid
        JWTService-->>AuthController: decoded payload
        AuthController-->>Microservice: 200 {valid: true, payload: {sub, email, role, ...}}
    else token invalid / expired / revoked
        JWTService-->>AuthController: error
        AuthController-->>Microservice: 401 {valid: false, error: "..."}
    end
```

---

## 6. Get Current User (/me)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant AuthController
    participant JWTService
    participant Redis
    participant DB

    Client->>AuthController: GET /api/v1/auth/me\nAuthorization: Bearer <access_token>

    AuthController->>JWTService: decode(token)
    JWTService->>Redis: check JTI denylist + user invalidation
    Redis-->>JWTService: not revoked
    JWTService-->>AuthController: payload {sub, ...}

    AuthController->>DB: SELECT auth_credentials WHERE id = sub
    DB-->>AuthController: AuthCredential

    alt account deactivated
        AuthController-->>Client: 401 Unauthorized
    end

    AuthController-->>Client: 200 {id, email, role, email_verified, created_at}
```

---

## 7. Change Password

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant AuthController
    participant JWTService
    participant Redis
    participant PasswordValidator
    participant HIBP
    participant DB
    participant TokenDenylist

    Client->>AuthController: PATCH /api/v1/auth/password\nAuthorization: Bearer <token>\n{current_password, new_password, new_password_confirmation}

    AuthController->>JWTService: decode(token)
    JWTService->>Redis: check revocation
    Redis-->>JWTService: ok
    JWTService-->>AuthController: payload

    AuthController->>DB: find AuthCredential
    DB-->>AuthController: credential

    AuthController-->>AuthController: authenticate(current_password) — bcrypt
    alt wrong current password
        AuthController-->>Client: 422 Current password is incorrect
    end

    alt passwords don't match
        AuthController-->>Client: 422 Passwords do not match
    end

    AuthController->>PasswordValidator: validate(new_password, email)
    PasswordValidator->>HIBP: k-anonymity breach check
    HIBP-->>PasswordValidator: result
    PasswordValidator->>DB: PasswordHistory.password_used?(credential, new_password)
    DB-->>PasswordValidator: true/false
    alt validation fails
        PasswordValidator-->>AuthController: errors
        AuthController-->>Client: 422 Unprocessable Entity
    end

    Note over AuthController,DB: Transaction begin
    AuthController->>DB: UPDATE auth_credentials SET password_digest = bcrypt(new_password)
    DB-->>DB: after_save callback → INSERT password_histories
    AuthController->>DB: UPDATE refresh_tokens SET revoked_at = now (all for user)
    Note over AuthController,DB: Transaction commit

    AuthController->>TokenDenylist: revoke!(current_jti, current_exp)
    TokenDenylist->>Redis: SETEX jti:denylist:{jti} {ttl} "1"

    AuthController->>TokenDenylist: invalidate_user_tokens!(user_id)
    TokenDenylist->>Redis: SETEX user:tokens:invalidated:{id} 960 {timestamp}

    AuthController-->>Client: 200 {message: "Password changed successfully"}
```

---

## 8. Forgot Password

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant RackAttack
    participant AuthController
    participant DB
    participant RabbitMQ

    Client->>RackAttack: POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password {email}
    RackAttack-->>RackAttack: check password-reset/ip (5/hour)
    RackAttack-->>RackAttack: check password-reset/email (5/hour)

        RabbitMQ-->>AuthController: ack

```

      parameter name: :per_page, in: :query, type: :integer, required: false,
                description: 'Results per page, 1-100 (default: 25)', example: 25

      parameter name: :page, in: :query, type: :integer, required: false,
                description: 'Page number (default: 1)', example: 1
      parameter name: :per_page, in: :query, type: :integer, required: false,

      response '200', 'Users listed' do
        schema type: :object,

                   type: :array,
                   items: { '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/admin_user' }

                 meta: {
                   type: :object,
                   properties: {
                     total: { type: :integer },
                     page: { type: :integer },
                     per_page: { type: :integer }
                   },
                   required: %w[total page per_page]
                 }
               }

        before do
          create_list(:auth_credential, 2)
          create(:auth_credential, email: 'alice@example.com')

        run_test!

      response '200', 'Users filtered by email search' do
        schema type: :object,

                 users: {

                   type: :object,
                   properties: {

                   properties: {
                     total: { type: :integer },
                     page: { type: :integer },

                   required: %w[total page per_page]

          expect(data['meta']['total']).to eq(0)
        end

        schema type: :object,

                 users: { type: :array, items: { '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/admin_user' } },

                   type: :object,
                   properties: {
                     total: { type: :integer },
                     page: { type: :integer },

content.text:
13/13 passing, rubocop clean. 

Root cause: `RSpec/LetSetup` auto-corrects by silently deleting `let!` variables that aren't referenced in test blocks — taking the data setup with them. Fix: reference `alice.email` and `bob.email` in assertions, which satisfies the cop and keeps the setup alive.

Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.

## Steps

1. Run these in parallel:
   - `git status` — see untracked and modified files
   - `git diff` — see unstaged changes
   - `git diff --cached` — see staged changes
   - `git log -5 --oneline` — learn commit message style of this repo

2. Analyze all changes. Understand what changed and why (infer from context).

3. Stage changed files by name (not `git add -A` or `git add .` — be precise, skip anything that looks like secrets or large binaries).

4. Write a commit message:
   - First line: imperative mood, under 72 chars, lowercase verb (e.g. "add", "fix", "refactor")
   - Body if needed: explain the why, not the what
   - **No `Co-Authored-By` line. No Claude attribution. Nothing about AI.**

5. Commit using a HEREDOC so formatting is preserved:
   ```
   git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   <message here>
   EOF
   )"
   ```

6. Push to the current branch's remote tracking branch:
   ```
   git push
   ```
   If no upstream set: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

7. Report: what was committed, the commit hash, push result. One short paragraph, no fluff.

## Constraints

- Never use `--no-verify`
- Never add `Co-Authored-By: Claude` or any AI attribution
- Never commit `.env`, credential files, or secrets
- If nothing to commit, say so and stop

Found the culprit. Look at `rails_helper.rb`:

```ruby
config.before(:suite) do
  DatabaseCleaner.clean_with(:truncation)  # truncates ALL tables before suite runs
end
```

This truncates every table before the test suite. If the container has `RAILS_ENV=development` set (overriding the `||= 'test'`), DatabaseCleaner hits `auth_db` instead of `auth_db_test`.

This is on me — I should have run rspec with an explicit env override:
```bash
docker-compose exec auth-api env RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rspec ...
```

Re-seed now:

docker-compose -f infrastructure/docker-compose.yml exec auth-api bundle exec rake db:seed
```

And I'll check how rspec is called in the project to make sure this doesn't happen again.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Commit Message Style

All commits must use **Conventional Commits** format:

```
<type>(<scope>): <subject>

[optional body]
```

**Types:** `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `style`, `perf`, `ci`, `build`

- Subject: imperative, lowercase, no period, ≤72 chars
- Scope: optional, use service/area name (e.g. `auth`, `jwt`, `rack-attack`)
- Body: explain *why*, not *what* — omit if subject is self-explanatory

Examples:
```
feat(auth): add JTI-based token revocation on logout
fix(controller): correct module namespace from Api to API
test(event-publisher): reset class-level connection between examples
chore(deps): add redis gem for rack-attack rate-limit backend
```

## Project Overview

**ThatCookiesMine** — a healthcare/medical appointment platform built as a monorepo of independent microservices. Each service has its own `.git` directory and is versioned independently.

## Services & Ports

| Service | Stack | Port | Status |
|---------|-------|------|--------|
| `auth-api/` | Ruby 3.3.6 + Rails 8.0 (API-only) | 3000 | Active |
| `admin/` | React 19 + Vite 7 + TypeScript 5.9 | 3001 | Active |
| `notification-service/` | Go 1.23 (RabbitMQ consumer) | — | Active |
| `core-api/` | Go 1.23 | 8080 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
| `frontend/` | React 19 + Node 22 LTS | 5173 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
| `mobile/` | TBD | — | Scaffolded |

Infrastructure: PostgreSQL 17 (two instances: auth on 5432, core on 5433), Redis 7.4, RabbitMQ 4.0, MailHog, Nginx reverse proxy on ports 80/443.

## Infrastructure / Docker

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Commit Message Style

All commits must use **Conventional Commits** format:

```
<type>(<scope>): <subject>

[optional body]
```

**Types:** `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `style`, `perf`, `ci`, `build`

- Subject: imperative, lowercase, no period, ≤72 chars
- Scope: optional, use service/area name (e.g. `auth`, `jwt`, `rack-attack`)
- Body: explain *why*, not *what* — omit if subject is self-explanatory

Examples:
```
feat(auth): add JTI-based token revocation on logout
fix(controller): correct module namespace from Api to API
test(event-publisher): reset class-level connection between examples
chore(deps): add redis gem for rack-attack rate-limit backend
```

## Project Overview

**ThatCookiesMine** — a healthcare/medical appointment platform built as a monorepo of independent microservices. Each service has its own `.git` directory and is versioned independently.

## Services & Ports

| Service | Stack | Port | Status |
|---------|-------|------|--------|
| `auth-api/` | Ruby 3.3.6 + Rails 8.0 (API-only) | 3000 | Active |
| `admin/` | React 19 + Vite 7 + TypeScript 5.9 | 3001 | Active |
| `notification-service/` | Go 1.23 (RabbitMQ consumer) | — | Active |
| `core-api/` | Go 1.23 | 8080 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
| `frontend/` | React 19 + Node 22 LTS | 5173 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
| `mobile/` | TBD | — | Scaffolded |

Infrastructure: PostgreSQL 17 (two instances: auth on 5432, core on 5433), Redis 7.4, RabbitMQ 4.0, MailHog, Nginx reverse proxy on ports 80/443.

## Infrastructure / Docker

```bash
cd infrastructure
./start-all.sh              # Start all services (recommended)
./start-infrastructure.sh   # Start only infra (DB, cache, broker)
./stop-all.sh               # Stop everything
docker-compose logs -f [service]
```

Dev access points when running:
- Auth API: http://localhost:3000 — Swagger docs at http://localhost:3000/api-docs
- Admin: http://localhost:3001
- RabbitMQ UI: http://localhost:15672
- MailHog: http://localhost:8025

## Auth API (Ruby/Rails)

```bash
cd auth-api
bundle install
cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up auth-api   # Start auth-api in Docker
bundle exec rspec                                          # All tests
bundle exec rspec spec/models/auth_credential_spec.rb     # Single file
COVERAGE=true bundle exec rspec                           # With coverage
bundle exec rubocop                                        # Lint
bundle exec rubocop -A                                     # Auto-fix
bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize                   # Regenerate OpenAPI docs
bundle exec rails db:create db:migrate
bundle exec rake tokens:cleanup                            # Maintenance task
```

### Auth API Architecture

- **Routes**: `config/routes.rb` → all endpoints under `/api/v1/auth/`
- **Controllers**: `app/controllers/api/v1/` — thin, delegate to services
- **Services**: `app/services/` — business logic layer (password policy, HIBP check, token management)
- **Models**: `AuthCredential` (users), `RefreshToken`, `PasswordHistory`, `PasswordResetToken`
- **JWT**: 15-min access tokens, 8-hour refresh tokens (30-min idle timeout)
- **Security**: NIST SP 800-63B password compliance, HIBP breach detection, rack-attack rate limiting, password history enforcement
- **Async**: RabbitMQ publishes to `notifications` topic exchange (routing key `email.password_reset`) — consumed by `notification-service`
- **Linting config**: `.rubocop.yml`

## Admin Dashboard (React/TypeScript)

```bash
cd admin
npm install
npm run dev       # Vite dev server on port 3001
npm run build     # TypeScript check + Vite production build
npm run lint      # ESLint
npm run preview   # Preview production build
```

### Admin Architecture

- **Entry**: `src/main.tsx` → `src/App.tsx` (React Router v7 routes)
- **Pages**: `src/pages/` — `login.tsx`, `dashboard.tsx`
- **API clients**: `src/api/client.ts` (Axios), `src/api/auth.ts`
- **Auth state**: `src/contexts/auth-context.tsx`
- **Guards**: `src/components/protected-route.tsx`
- **UI**: shadcn/ui components; forms use react-hook-form + Zod

## Notification Service (Go)

Background worker that consumes email events from RabbitMQ and sends emails via MailHog (dev) / SMTP (prod).

```bash
cd notification-service
go build ./...
go vet ./...
# Run via Docker (no standalone dev server):
cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up notification-service
```

### Notification Service Architecture

- **Entry**: `cmd/main.go` — starts consumer with retry loop (up to 10 attempts, 5s delay)
- **Consumer**: `internal/consumer/consumer.go` — connects to RabbitMQ, binds queue `notification-service.email` to `notifications` exchange with routing key `email.*`
- **Handlers**: `internal/handlers/password_reset.go` — handles `email.password_reset` event type
- **Mailer**: `internal/mailer/mailer.go` — sends email via SMTP

## Dev Setup

Scripts in `dev-setup/` for bootstrapping a local multi-repo environment:

```bash
cd dev-setup
./repos-setup.sh    # Clone all service repos
./repos-update.sh   # Pull latest changes across all repos
./tmux-repos.sh     # Launch tmux sessions per repo with dev windows
```

## Container-First Rule

**Never install dependencies on the local machine.** All runtimes, gems, packages, and tools run inside Docker containers.

- No `bundle install`, `npm install`, `go get` locally
- No local `ruby`, `node`, `go` version management (rbenv, nvm, etc.) for running code
- Run commands via `docker-compose exec <service> <cmd>` or inside the container
- Tests, linters, migrations — all through Docker

```bash
# Run rspec inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rspec

# Run rubocop inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rubocop

# Rails tasks inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rails db:migrate
```

### Updating Gemfile.lock (Ruby)

After editing `Gemfile`, regenerate `Gemfile.lock` on disk using the image before rebuilding — never skip this step or bundler will error on mismatch.

```bash
# Run BEFORE docker compose build whenever Gemfile changes
docker run --rm -v /path/to/auth-api:/app infrastructure-auth-api bundle lock

# Then rebuild
docker compose build auth-api
```

## API Testing

Bruno collections in `bruno/auth-api/` — use Bruno (not Postman) for manual API testing. Environments defined in `bruno/auth-api/environments/`.

## New Endpoint Rule

After adding any new endpoint to auth-api, always:

1. Write a rswag integration spec in `spec/integration/authentication_spec.rb` documenting the endpoint
2. Regenerate OpenAPI docs:
```bash
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize
```

Never ship a new endpoint without updating the OpenAPI spec.

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# RAILS HELPER - Rails-Specific RSpec Configuration
# =============================================================================
# This file loads Rails and configures testing helpers.
# Most specs will require this file (via `require 'rails_helper'`).

# Ensure we're in test environment — abort hard if container env overrides this.
# The auth-api Docker container sets RAILS_ENV=development; running rspec without
# an explicit override would connect DatabaseCleaner to auth_db and truncate it.
if ENV['RAILS_ENV'] && ENV['RAILS_ENV'] != 'test'
  abort "ERROR: rspec must run with RAILS_ENV=test (got '#{ENV['RAILS_ENV']}'). " \
        'Run: RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rspec'
end

ENV['RAILS_ENV'] = 'test'

# Load Rails application
require_relative '../config/environment'

# Prevent running tests if database migrations are pending
raise 'The test database is not empty' if Rails.env.production?

require 'rspec/rails'
require 'database_cleaner/active_record'

# ===========================================================================
# LOAD SUPPORT FILES
# ===========================================================================
# Load all files in spec/support/ directory
# These contain shared helpers, configurations, and custom matchers
Rails.root.glob('spec/support/**/*.rb').each { |f| require f }

# ===========================================================================
# DATABASE SETUP
# ===========================================================================

begin
  # Check that migrations are current
  ActiveRecord::Migration.maintain_test_schema!
rescue ActiveRecord::PendingMigrationError => e
  raise e.to_s.strip
end

# ===========================================================================
# RSPEC CONFIGURATION
# ===========================================================================

BCrypt::Engine.cost = BCrypt::Engine::MIN_COST

RSpec.configure do |config|
  # ==========================================================================
  # TIME HELPERS
  # ==========================================================================
  # Include ActiveSupport time helpers (freeze_time, travel, travel_to)
  # Used for testing time-sensitive features like token expiration
  config.include ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers

  # ==========================================================================
  # FACTORY BOT
  # ==========================================================================
  # Include FactoryBot methods without namespace
  # Instead of: FactoryBot.create(:user)
  # You can use: create(:user)
  config.include FactoryBot::Syntax::Methods

  # ==========================================================================
  # HOST HEADER
  # ==========================================================================
  # Rails HostAuthorization allows .localhost and .test by default.
  # RSpec integration sessions default to www.example.com which is blocked.
  config.before(:each, type: :request) { host! 'localhost' }

  # ==========================================================================
  # EXTERNAL SERVICE STUBS
  # ==========================================================================
  # Stub Have I Been Pwned API to avoid external calls in tests
  config.before do
    allow(PasswordBreachChecker).to receive(:breached?).and_return(false)
  end

  # Stub TokenDenylist so tests don't require a live Redis connection.
  # Individual tests that assert revocation behaviour should override these stubs.
  config.before do
    allow(TokenDenylist).to receive(:revoke!)
    allow(TokenDenylist).to receive(:invalidate_user_tokens!)
    allow(TokenDenylist).to receive_messages(revoked?: false, user_tokens_invalidated?: false)
  end

  # ==========================================================================
  # DATABASE CLEANING
  # ==========================================================================
  # Ensure each test starts with a clean database

  # Use transactions for most tests (fast, auto-rollback)
  config.use_transactional_fixtures = true

  # For tests that need real database commits (e.g., JavaScript tests),
  # use DatabaseCleaner gem with truncation strategy
  config.before(:suite) do
    DatabaseCleaner.strategy = :transaction
    DatabaseCleaner.clean_with(:truncation)
  end

  config.around do |example|
    DatabaseCleaner.cleaning do
      example.run
    end
  end

  # ==========================================================================
  # SPEC TYPE INFERENCE
  # ==========================================================================
  # Automatically determine spec type from file location
  # spec/models/ → type: :model
  # spec/requests/ → type: :request
  # spec/services/ → type: :service
  config.infer_spec_type_from_file_location!

  # ==========================================================================
  # FILTERING
  # ==========================================================================
  # Filter Rails framework backtrace from error output
  # Makes it easier to see YOUR code in error traces
  config.filter_rails_from_backtrace!
end

Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.

## Steps

1. Run these in parallel:
   - `git status` — see untracked and modified files
   - `git diff` — see unstaged changes
   - `git diff --cached` — see staged changes
   - `git log -5 --oneline` — learn commit message style of this repo

2. Analyze all changes. Understand what changed and why (infer from context).

3. Stage changed files by name (not `git add -A` or `git add .` — be precise, skip anything that looks like secrets or large binaries).

4. Write a commit message:
   - First line: imperative mood, under 72 chars, lowercase verb (e.g. "add", "fix", "refactor")
   - Body if needed: explain the why, not the what
   - **No `Co-Authored-By` line. No Claude attribution. Nothing about AI.**

5. Commit using a HEREDOC so formatting is preserved:
   ```
   git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   <message here>
   EOF
   )"
   ```

6. Push to the current branch's remote tracking branch:
   ```
   git push
   ```
   If no upstream set: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

7. Report: what was committed, the commit hash, push result. One short paragraph, no fluff.

## Constraints

- Never use `--no-verify`
- Never add `Co-Authored-By: Claude` or any AI attribution
- Never commit `.env`, credential files, or secrets
- If nothing to commit, say so and stop
